_meta.name: CLM r5 full training launch state — 2026-04-20
_meta.purpose: dest1_clm P2 substrate launch; dual-track parallel with ALM r11
_meta.prior_state: shared/state/clm_r5_parallel_20260420.json (UNBLOCKED on CPU BLAS, gated on r5-a6.5 for GPU)

state_reason: r5-a10.5-A + r5-a10.5-C BOTH landed (see clm_r5_a10p5_leak_fix_20260420.json for full audit). Step 1 wallclock 16+min → ~60s (>=16x improvement) by porting decoder_backward block-loop d² scalar outer-product accumulation to BLAS via _matmul_At. Step 0 metrics IDENTICAL (ce=5.54523, phi_holo=558.583). However wallclock projection at ~60s/step × 20K steps = 14 days on hetzner CPU — still infeasible. Full 20K launch BLOCKED but blocker shifted: NOT the leak (Phase E arena would help but is sub-dominant), NOT the wallclock-per-step (C fixed it), but the 14-day total runtime on CPU substrate. Production path: pivot to GPU via libhxcuda dispatch (r5-a6.5) so 170M→1B can run on ubu RTX 5070 or H100. Original launch (pid 1690453, 19:09:17Z) DIED at 20:01:52Z via OOM-killer (~2 GB/min leak rate, now resolved by A+C fixes — secondary leak source).

launch_decision_audit.original_target: CLM 1B r5 full (1.51B params, bf16, 50000 steps)
launch_decision_audit.decision: DOWNGRADED to CLM 170M r5 full (106M params, 20000 steps)
rationale: 1B hetzner CPU smoke (batch=2 seq=512): RSS peaked at 81GB/128GB + swap 34GB/63GB — thrashing confirmed, OOM risk on 20K+ step run

Ubuntu RTX 5070: 11.5GB VRAM available BUT (a) no ~/Dev/anima clone, (b) no libhxblas.so / libhxlmhead.so built under ~/Dev/hexa-lang/self/native/build/, (c) trainer has ZERO CUDA call sites — would still CPU-pin on 16GB system RAM. Blocked on r5-a6.5 libhxcuda dispatch.
New H100 pod: REJECTED — 2-pod cap (87xscivuggrwvk ALM r11 running, hhzla1nxmp5019 exited). Also trainer has no CUDA path — $ burn for zero GPU engagement.
170M hetzner CPU BLAS: CONFIRMED feasible. 3-step smoke RSS ~13GB (10.5% of 128GB), steady-state ~90s/step at 243%% CPU multi-core BLAS.
feedback_safe_path_auto + feedback_auto_recommend + feedback_no_ask_just_do: take the working path now, document 1B blocker for follow-up.

host.chosen: hetzner (157.180.8.154, AMD Ryzen 9 7950X3D, 128GB RAM, 32 cores, no GPU)
host.reason: Only host where trainer binary runs with multi-core BLAS engaged AND memory fits model+optimizer working set

substrate.honors_policy: feedback_no_resume_data_change — step 0 cold start, no prior params

run.corpus: data/corpus.txt (5.24 MB cycled) — but binary defaults to /workspace/corpus_clm_r4.txt (NOT FOUND) so trainer falls through to LCG fallback batch path; r5-a10 must wire --corpus CLI override or read corpus.path from config JSON
run.save_every_rationale: RELAUNCH BUDGETED — leak rate ~2GB/min limits feasible runtime to ~45 min before 90GB watchdog. 200 steps × ~30s/step (batch=1 seq=256) = 100 min nominal; watchdog will SIGTERM at 30 min OR RSS≥90GB, whichever first. save_every=50 emits up to 4 ckpts before kill — diagnostic milestones for dest1_clm AN11 probe.
run.watchdog_script: /tmp/clm_r5_relaunch.sh (90 GB RSS hard-kill + 30 min walltime cap)
notes: DEAD prior run: pid 1690453 OOM-killed 2026-04-19T20:01:52 +0200 (UTC: 18:01:52). dmesg: total-vm:274290872kB anon-rss:117790324kB pgtables:400528kB swapents:16456000.

ROOT CAUSE: train_step() per-step leaks compound — (a) _current_corpus_desc() calls corpus_open() every batch (println + wc -c exec), (b) _c41_gwt_head_new + _c41_holo_head_new alloc d_model² matrices every step uncached, (c) decoder_forward/backward tensor lists accumulate via hexa list pass-by-value push pattern with no reclaim. Combined leak rate ~2 GB/min observed.
ARCHIVED: prior ckpt_dir → /tmp/ckpt_clm_r5_170m_dead_20260419/ (step_0 + meta retained as baseline). Prior log → /tmp/clm_r5_full_dead_20260419.log.

SMOKE: batch=1 seq=256 5-step smoke (pid 2533287) reached step 0 OK + save OK, then ran 3min on step 1 forward without finishing (RSS 14→17GB / VSZ 30GB), confirming step 1 wallclock >>3min and leak persists at smaller config. Killed before completing.
RELAUNCH NOT --resume: feedback_no_resume_data_change honored — fresh ckpt dir, step 0 from cold init.

step0_metrics.clm_p4_2_gate_phi_holo_gt_500: DEPRECATED_PHANTOM — absolute 500 gate trivially met at random init (raw MI 0.00142 × N=393216 = 558; N-independent gate required)

clm_p4_2_gate_phi_holo_ratio.status: PENDING — evaluate at step_3000 / step_10000 / step_20000 when per-step phi_holo logging restored (r5-a10 followup)

first_3_steps.note: hexa println multi-arg bug masks step 1/2 log lines; only step=0 emits via explicit str() concat path (a63a0393 fix)
first_3_steps.followup: r5-a10 candidate: retrofit _holo_log_line() helper to use str() concat for every println currently using variadic form (approx 6 call sites in train loop)

eta.wall_clock_note: CPU-only on hetzner — very slow. Acceptable as DEST1_CLM P2 stub substrate ONLY. r5-a6.5 + GPU host will cut this ~100x.

blocker_for_1b.desc: r5-a6.5 (HXBLAS_CUDA=1 cuBLAS dispatch shim) LANDED 2026-04-20 02:09 KST (commit 08fa0e76). H100 smoke 2026-04-20 ~20:30Z confirmed dispatch wires correctly with 300+ real cuBLAS calls/step + bit-identical numerical output. HOWEVER speedup vs CPU is only 1.037x — see shared/state/clm_r5_gpu_dispatch_smoke_20260420.json. New blocker r5-a6.6: persistent device buffer pool in shim + r5-a10.5-E persistent step-scoped raw arena in train_clm.hexa _matmul/_matmul_At. Without these, GPU host buys ~3.7% — not enough for 1B economics.
blocker_for_1b.blocks: 1B / 3B / 7B training on any GPU host (ubu RTX 5070 OR H100 pod)
followup_order: r5-a6.5: LANDED (libhxblas_cuda.so dispatch verified via H100 smoke)
r5-a6.6: NEW — persistent device buffer pool in hxblas_cuda_shim.c (eliminate per-call cudaMalloc/Free)
r5-a10.5-E: NEW — train_clm.hexa _matmul step-scoped raw arena (eliminate per-call alloc_raw/free_raw + list↔raw marshal). Largest single lever — addresses dominant cost.
r5-a8: rsync anima + libs to ubu, smoke 1-step with HXBLAS_CUDA=1 — DEFERRED, ubu unreachable (LAN+tailscale both fail), H100 used as proxy
r5-a9: 1B full launch on ubu OR fresh H100 — BLOCKED on r5-a6.6 + r5-a10.5-E speedup (need >3x not 1.037x)
r5-a10: retrofit println multi-arg → str() concat across train_clm.hexa

phi_vec_laws_attachment.phi_vec_status_at_launch: stub (phi_cache_load skipped) — r5-a10 follow-up to wire real cache emit
phi_vec_laws_attachment.an11_gate_status: PARTIAL — step 0 phi_holo=558.583 (baseline locked); absolute >500 gate deprecated phantom (N-dependent). Ratio gate phi_holo(N)/phi_holo(0) >= 1.2 PENDING multi-step telemetry (r5-a10 per-step logging). phi_vec per-step emission also pending r5-a10.

safety_checks.one_shot_best: CPU path is compromised but optimal within CPU-BLAS envelope

next_checkpoints: T+75h (approx): step 10000 ckpt — eval_ce + phi_holo check
T+250h (approx): step 20000 ckpt — final substrate, R2 upload, dest1_clm AN11 probe
PARALLEL: author r5-a6.5 libhxcuda.so dispatch in anima/docs + land in training/train_clm.hexa — unblocks 1B real launch

revision_history.event: initial launch CLI had --save-every 5000 == CLI default → config.save_every=100 overrode → ckpt flood. KILLED, cleaned.
relaunched with --save-every 10000 (!= CLI default) to force CLI-precedence. 2 saves total.
OOM-KILLED pid 1690453 after +52min. anon-rss 117GB. ~2 GB/min leak from per-step corpus_open + c41 head alloc + decoder tensor accum. Only step_0 ckpt produced.
smoke batch=1 seq=256 5-step (pid 2533287) — 14→17GB RSS in 3min, step 1 forward unfinished, killed. Confirmed leak persists at smallest viable config.

RELAUNCH pid 2569927 + watchdog pid 2569924. Budgeted: steps=200 batch=1 seq=256 save_every=50, RSS≥90GB hard-kill, 30min walltime cap. Goal: extract diagnostic ckpts step_50/100/150/200 for dest1_clm AN11 substrate probe before next OOM. r5-a10 leak fix queued (separate commit, train_clm.hexa code work).

Watchdog pid 2569927 SIGKILLed at +8m30s by r5-a10 follow-up agent. Diagnostic data captured: only step 0 logged + saved (1.15GB ckpt), trainer was stuck mid-step-1 compute the entire 8.5min. RSS curve linear: 8.65GB→36.3GB over 8.5min = 3.4GB/min. step_1 wallclock >> 8min on hetzner CPU BLAS at batch=1 seq=256 — confirms compute is also unviable, not just memory.

r5-a10 LANDED + smoke retry. Three leak fixes applied (see clm_r5_a10_leak_fix_20260420.json): (a) _current_corpus_desc() module-scope cache eliminates per-step exec(wc -c) + println, (b) c41 GWT/Holo heads cached at module scope (one-shot deterministic seed init), (c) _c41_phi_holo rewritten to use _tz pre-allocation + index assignment instead of nested .push() loops. Build OK (479KB binary). Smoke @ batch=2 seq=512: leak rate dropped 3.4GB/min → ~1.5GB/min (steady), step 0 reached at t=75s (vs prior never), but step 1 still incomplete after 3min.

r5-a10 SMOKE FAIL — residual leak 1.5GB/min still > 200MB/min PASS gate. FULL LAUNCH BLOCKED per task spec rule. Root cause of residual: hexa runtime list operations (push/_tz init) leak across the decoder_forward 12-block stack + remaining c41/sw inline aux paths. r5-a10.5 needed: deeper hot-path refactor (likely decoder_forward block-level _tz reuse, _block_attn intermediate buffer pool). State: r5_a10_followup → r5_a10_LANDED_PARTIAL_FAIL.

r5_a10_followup.title: train_clm.hexa per-step leak hardening
r5_a10_followup.scope: training/train_clm.hexa edits — code regression separate from this ops launch
fixes_applied: (a) _current_corpus_desc() module-scope cache via G_CORPUS_INIT guard — eliminates per-step exec(wc -c) + println
(b) C41 GWT + Holo heads cached at module scope (G_C41_INIT guard + flat field arrays G_C41_GWT_PROJ/GATE/HOLO_SCRAM) — one-shot deterministic seed init per d_model
(c) _c41_phi_holo body rewritten: _tz pre-allocation + index assignment replaces nested .push() loops; inlined holo encode skips unused first `db` portion of bulk; ~983k pushes/step eliminated
fixes_deferred: (d) --corpus CLI override + JSON corpus.path read — already present (line 4222-4228), corpus path issue was missing file on host (resolved by symlink /root/Dev/anima/data/corpus.txt → /workspace/corpus_clm_r4.txt)
(e) per-step println str() concat retrofit — orthogonal to leak, deferred to separate commit

residual_blocker.title: Hexa runtime list-op leak in decoder_forward + aux paths
residual_blocker.evidence: Even with c41 head + corpus cache + phi_holo refactor, RSS still grows 1.5GB/min steady-state during step 1. _block_forward calls _tz / _tslice / _tadd / _matmul each layer; 12 layers × 2 batches × ~10 list creates per call = ~240 fresh list allocations per step. Plus residual aux paths (gwt_broadcast_reduce, hidden_consensus, sw_qc_loss).
next_steps_options: Option A: Profile hexa runtime to find which list op leaks most (instrument _tz, push, _tslice). Likely _tz is the culprit since it's `_tz(n) := for n: out.push(0.0)`.
Option B: Replace _tz with a hexa-runtime-native zero-fill primitive that does single alloc instead of N pushes.
Option C: Move decoder_forward / c41 heads to FFI (call C-side _matmul that uses already-existing alloc_raw/free_raw discipline).
Option D: Wait for r5-a6.5 libhxcuda dispatch + run on H100 GPU where allocation is on device (CPU RSS not affected).
residual_blocker.blocks: Full 20K-step CLM r5 170M training on hetzner CPU BLAS. ALM r11 H100 path unaffected.

r5_a10_followup.blocks: Full 20K-step CLM r5 170M training; any 1B+ launch on any host
